panels.title: Read this first, model names, chat modes, content, and spend

options.content:
# Sessions and model labels

This dashboard separates what is **real local telemetry** from what is **planning-only**.

- `gen_ai_request_model` and `gen_ai_response_model` are the exact model labels emitted by the GitHub Copilot runtime. They may be internal, preview, routed, or resolved labels. Treat them as telemetry labels, not friendly product names.
- Local Prometheus currently exposes `gen_ai_operation_name=chat`, but not a clean dashboard label for Ask/Edit/Agent/Plan mode. When emitted, mode-like details are in Tempo span attributes such as `copilot_chat.request.shape`, not in the Prometheus metric labels.
- Content capture is visible in trace attributes in Aspire or Tempo, for example `gen_ai.input.messages`, `gen_ai.output.messages`, `gen_ai.tool.call.arguments`, and `gen_ai.tool.call.result`. Do not put raw content in Grafana dashboards by default.
- Spend per session is only an estimate if you register local prices. It is not official GitHub billing or AI Credits reconciliation.

panels.title: Telemetry model labels by session and token type
panels.description: These are the model labels emitted by telemetry, grouped by session. They are the best local source for model usage, but not necessarily the UI-friendly model name.

targets.expr: topk(50, sum by (session_id, gen_ai_request_model, gen_ai_response_model, gen_ai_token_type, workspace_path_hash) (increase(gen_ai_client_token_usage_sum{service_name="copilot-chat"}[$__range])))

panels.title: Token volume by telemetry model label
Real AIU consumed (AI credits, workspace)
panels.description: Real AI Units reported by GitHub Copilot telemetry for workspace sessions in the selected range. AIU = copilot_chat.copilot_usage_nano_aiu / 1e9. This is the real AI-credits-equivalent signal. Official AI Credits billing still comes from GitHub usage or billing exports.

panels.title: Estimated local spend by model (USD what-if, planning prices)
panels.description: Token counts per model multiplied by local planning prices. Prices are local assumptions (price_source=local-planning-assumption), not official billing and not provider-confirmed for these labels. Official spend and AI Credits come from GitHub billing or usage exports. Use register-model-price.sh or seed-model-prices.sh to set your own source-of-truth prices.

targets.expr: sum by (gen_ai_request_model) ((increase(gen_ai_client_token_usage_sum{service_name="copilot-chat"}[$__range]) / 1e6) * on (gen_ai_request_model, gen_ai_token_type) group_left() max by (gen_ai_request_model, gen_ai_token_type) (copilot_model_price_usd_per_million_ratio))

panels.title: How to see content capture safely

options.content:
## Content capture

Content capture is not shown in Grafana dashboards by default because it may include prompts, file snippets, tool arguments, and tool results.

Use one of these paths:

1. **Aspire:** open `http://localhost:18888`, go to Traces, select a `copilot-chat` trace, open a `chat` or `execute_tool` span, inspect attributes like `gen_ai.input.messages`, `gen_ai.output.messages`, `gen_ai.tool.call.arguments`, and `gen_ai.tool.call.result`.

2. **Grafana Tempo Explore:** open Explore, choose Tempo, query `{service.name="copilot-chat"}`, select a trace, inspect span attributes.

3. **CLI safe inspection:** use `$HOME/frontier-cockpit/local-otel/show-trace-content.sh` to list content-capture keys without printing raw content. Use `--show-content` only in trusted local demos.

For customer environments, keep content capture off unless the customer explicitly approves it.

panels.title: Official model multipliers, what this is and is not

options.content:
# Official premium-request multipliers

The multiplier per model comes from the official GitHub Copilot pricing docs and is registered locally with `register-model-multiplier.sh` / `seed-model-multipliers.sh`. It is published as `copilot_model_premium_request_multiplier_ratio`, kept alive by the `com.frontier.copilot-otel-model-registry` LaunchAgent.

- **Multiplier value is official.** Source: [GitHub model multipliers, legacy annual plans](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/copilot-billing/request-based-billing-legacy/model-multipliers-for-annual-plans). These are the legacy annual request-based multipliers. GitHub moved to usage-based billing on 2026-06-01, so they apply only to subscribers on a legacy annual plan and are subject to change.
- **The request count is local telemetry, an estimate.** The table below multiplies the number of `chat` LLM calls per model by the multiplier. Agent mode makes several model calls per user prompt, so this **over-counts** versus GitHub's per-user-request billing. Use it for relative model-cost comparison, not invoice reconciliation.
- **Official premium-request totals and AI Credits still come from GitHub billing exports or the Copilot usage metrics API.** This panel never replaces them.
- Rows are limited to `model_key_kind="telemetry_label"`, the models actually observed locally. The full official catalog is also registered under `model_key_kind="official_catalog"` for reference.

panels.title: Estimated premium-request-equivalents by model (local estimate x official multiplier)
panels.description: LLM chat calls per model multiplied by the official premium-request multiplier. The multiplier is official, the call count is local telemetry, and agent mode over-counts versus per-user-request billing. Planning aid only, not official billing.

targets.expr: sum by (gen_ai_request_model) (increase(gen_ai_client_operation_duration_count{service_name="copilot-chat"}[$__range])) * on (gen_ai_request_model) group_left() max by (gen_ai_request_model) (copilot_model_premium_request_multiplier_ratio{model_key_kind="telemetry_label"})

title: GitHub Copilot Sessions and Model Labels (Local)
